articles.title: American Culture Quiz: Test yourself on Founding Fathers and vaccine victories
articles.summary: The American Culture Quiz is a weekly test of our national traits, trends, history and people. This time, test your knowledge of Founding Fathers, vaccine victories and more.

articles.title: Expert who fled Cuba warns of ‘vicious cycle’ that will lead to ‘communists in double digits’ in Congress
articles.summary: Mike Gonzalez, who fled communist Cuba, warns a vicious cycle of socialist candidates winning could put communists in double digits in Congress.

articles.title: Homeowners terrorized by pro squatters turning dreams into nightmares in palm-lined paradise, experts say
articles.summary: Professional squatters allegedly use forged deeds, fake leases and identity theft to hijack Los Angeles homes, prompting calls for a dedicated task force.

articles.title: As America marks 250 years, our greatest chapters are still ahead of us
articles.summary: Founders fire — the bold vision and risk-taking spirit behind the Declaration of Independence — still drives American exceptionalism 250 years later.

articles.title: 9/11 Museum CEO reflects on lasting impact of terror attacks as America marks 250th birthday

articles.title: Before the Boston Tea Party, there were New Hampshire's Pine Tree Riots
articles.summary: Gov. Kelly Ayotte says the Pine Tree Riot in New Hampshire predated the Boston Tea Party and may have inspired the famous colonial protest.

articles.title: Revolutionary War cannons debut at Savannah History Museum for America 250
articles.summary: Nineteen cannons and a dozen or so small artifacts recovered from Savannah River find a new home inside the Savannah History Museum.

articles.title: The story behind Betsy Ross and the first American flag as America celebrates 250th birthday
articles.summary: Betsy Ross is credited with sewing the first American flag in 1776 from her home in Philadelphia, while the colonies were still under British rule.

articles.title: New Jersey coastal city clashes over proposal to transform amusement park into luxury hotel
articles.summary: A 5-2 council vote rezoned the former Gillian's Wonderland Pier site for rehabilitation, but a planned lawsuit challenges the boardwalk hotel proposal.

articles.title: Trump set to deliver 'historic' speech celebrating America's 250th anniversary
articles.summary: President Donald Trump is set to deliver a historic speech on the National Mall featuring stories of American heroes before a massive fireworks display.

articles.title: Kylian Mbappé's seventh goal of the World Cup lifts France past Paraguay in physical Round of 16 match
articles.summary: Kylian Mbappé drilled a second-half penalty for his 19th career World Cup goal, tying Lionel Messi for the tournament lead against Paraguay.

articles.summary: Authorities ruled out DUI in the Paul Pelosi hit-and-run, but the sheriff's office is submitting a driver re-evaluation referral to the California DMV.

articles.title: City of Philadelphia may have leaked some MLB All-Stars hours before the big announcement
articles.summary: Light pole banners spotted around Philadelphia appear to reveal MLB All-Star Game rosters early, including players such as Matt Olson and Paul Skenes.

articles.title: Egypt national team manager waves Palestinian flag after World Cup win over Australia

articles.summary: From Colombia to Peru, a security-driven right wing is sweeping the region as Bukele-style politics becomes the hemisphere's most important export.

articles.title: The '1776 Diet': What Americans really ate during the nation's founding
articles.summary: Colonial Americans ate whole foods, organ meats and johnnycakes out of necessity. A registered dietician weighed in on what is worth emulating today.

articles.title: Trump pardons 9 people convicted of tampering with emission controls on diesel engines
articles.summary: Trump pardoned people convicted of Clean Air Act violations who he said were "persecuted by the Biden Administration" and punished for "fixing their car."

articles.title: Pope Leo urges US to welcome immigrants in America 250 speech before visiting global migrant hot spot
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV accepted the 2026 Liberty Medal virtually from the Vatican before departing for Lampedusa to urge Europe to accept more migrants.

articles.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman wants to negotiate a 5% stake in company for US if competitors agree to key provision
articles.summary: Sam Altman reportedly met with Trump and Bernie Sanders about offering 5% of OpenAI to the public, but only if Anthropic and Meta do the same.

articles.title: North Carolina governor faces criticism for skipping Trump-backed America 250 event
articles.summary: Chuck Edwards accused Gov. Josh Stein of choosing partisanship over patriotism by boycotting the Great American State Fair on the National Mall.

articles.title: Top Trump official touts how DC makeover is proof America is rejecting 'decline by choice'
articles.summary: Doug Burgum defended a Washington, D.C., infrastructure push, citing 510 graffiti removals, monument restoration and crime reduction ahead of July 4th.

articles.title: Pro-Trump artist unleashes powerful message about American flag, touts massive Old Glory painting
articles.summary: A Trump-supporting artist is painting a potentially record-breaking 9-by-17-foot American flag at the Great American State Fair but insists the flag belongs to everyone.

articles.title: Newsom, Walz urge Congress to block anti-climate bill in their ‘woke’ crusade
articles.summary: Democratic governors urge Congress to reject the Stop Climate Shakedowns Act as the Supreme Court prepares to hear the Boulder ExxonMobil lawsuit.

articles.summary: Kris Ramsingh, a Trinidad-born Virginia businessowner, calls birthright citizenship a "slap in the face" to legal immigrants who worked hard.

articles.title: House Dems dodge questions on SCOTUS trans athlete ruling, shift blame to 'culture wars'
articles.summary: House Democrats mostly sidestepped questions on the Supreme Court transgender athletes ruling, pivoting to the economy and Washington dysfunction.

articles.title: Al Green responds to July 4 question with renewed call to impeach Trump
articles.summary: Democratic lawmakers affirm pride in American democracy and freedoms while voicing concerns about the administration ahead of the 250th Independence Day.

articles.title: Trump hails America as 'most exceptional nation ever to exist' in Mount Rushmore speech
articles.summary: President Trump declared communism the greatest threat facing the U.S. and vowed the country would never become a communist nation in his Mount Rushmore speech.

articles.title: Trump grants pardons to 'persecuted' mechanics in right-to-repair crackdown: 'I am setting them all free'
articles.summary: Trump granted full pardons to more than half a dozen people, calling the prosecutions "weaponization."

articles.title: Mamdani blasts ICE agents, Elon Musk and 'supremacy' in America 250 speech ahead of July 4 weekend

articles.title: Trump calls out NATO ahead of summit, calling it 'ridiculous' for US to persist on 'one sided path'
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump blasted NATO on Truth Social over defense spending disparities ahead of the alliance's upcoming summit in Ankara, Turkey.

articles.title: Cruz pushes bill to hold tax-exempt sponsors accountable as DOJ probes Singham network
articles.summary: Ted Cruz pushes the SPONSOR Act to strip tax-exempt status from nonprofits funding political violence as the DOJ launches a grand jury probe.

articles.title: Democratic senate candidate called for mass release of criminals during prison abolition webinar
articles.summary: Abdul El-Sayed called for decarceration and abolishing prisons in an unearthed 2020 webinar, raising concerns about his Michigan Senate race bid.

articles.title: Republican unveils Declaration of Independence bill ahead of America's 250th birthday
articles.summary: Rep. Matt Van Epps said his bill reaffirming the Declaration of Independence directly responds to socialist candidates gaining ground in primaries.

articles.title: We asked Americans to grade the economy. Then we asked if it would change their vote.
articles.summary: Americans at the Great American State Fair gave the economy a grade B but said values, faith, family and party affiliation matter more at the ballot box.

articles.title: James Carville breaks with famous ‘It’s the economy, stupid’ slogan because of Trump
articles.summary: James Carville says his famous 1992 catchphrase "It's the economy, stupid" now haunts him because of what he calls Trump's corruption today.

articles.title: Vulnerable House Dem's bipartisanship push clashes with fantasy to ‘beat the s--- out of’ Hegseth
articles.summary: Rep. Susie Lee, D-Nev., who is up for re-election in a crucial swing-district, said she wanted to “beat the s--- out of" Pete Hegseth after touting bipartisanship during an address to Nevadans.

articles.title: World leaders, dignitaries pay tribute to America on historic 250th birthday
articles.summary: King Charles and Pope Leo XIV were among world leaders sending messages to Trump on America's 250th Independence Day. From Jerusalem to the UAE and Japan congratulations were sent.

articles.title: Conservative Keiko Fujimori officially declared winner of Peru's presidential runoff election
articles.summary: Keiko Fujimori won Peru's presidential runoff after defeating Roberto Sánchez by a razor-thin margin, pledging an "iron fist" against surging crime.

articles.title: Waltz calls out Iranian diplomat at UN following drone strikes on Bahrain and Kuwait
articles.summary: Mike Waltz held up photos of drone and missile strike damage in Bahrain during a tense U.N. Security Council exchange with Iranian diplomat Iravani.

articles.title: Experts ‘deeply’ concerned over Iran’s work at underground nuclear site
articles.summary: Pickaxe Mountain, a secret nuclear site in the Zagros Mountains, shows continued construction, according to satellite imagery. IAEA inspectors have not been allowed to visit.

articles.title: Interpol issues red notice for Ukrainian woman wanted for Monaco apartment bombing targeting oligarch
articles.summary: Interpol issued a red notice for Anastasiia Berezovska, suspected of planting an explosive device targeting sanctioned oligarch Vadym Yermolaiev in Monaco.

articles.title: Couple publicly caned after alleged TikTok kiss sparks outrage in Indonesia
articles.summary: A viral TikTok livestream led to a public caning for a young couple in Indonesia's Aceh province after they were convicted of kissing during a TikTok livestream.

articles.title: Six Kurdish fighters killed in IRGC ambush as clashes spread across western Iran
articles.summary: The PDKI says six Kurdish fighters were killed in an Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corp ambush near Piranshahr, while the Revolutionary Guards claim five Kurdish militants are dead.

articles.title: 11-year-old dies from rabies after bat landed on his face while he was sleeping
articles.summary: An 11-year-old boy in Ontario died of rabies after a bat landed on his face as he slept, according to the Canadian Medical Association Journal.

articles.title: Security guard survives eight days beneath collapsed shopping center after Venezuela earthquakes
articles.summary: Video shows Hernán Alberto Gil Flores carried through cheering crowds after eight days trapped in a collapsed shopping center in La Guaira, Venezuela.

articles.title: Russia unleashes nearly 600 missiles and drones on Kyiv in deadliest strike since May
articles.summary: Ukraine’s U.N. ambassador called Russia a “pariah state" after Moscow launched one of its heaviest overnight attacks on Kyiv, killing at least 18 people and wounding more than 90 people.

articles.title: UN chief backs official who reposted anti-Israel activist's claims amid calls for investigation
articles.summary: United Nations official Vanessa Frazier faces criticism for sharing unverified claims from a woman facing terror charges linked to Hamas on social media.

articles.title: After 1,000 days of war: Many Israeli children carry trauma into summer break
articles.summary: Israeli children begin summer vacation still coping with war trauma, as 84% reportedly showed signs of emotional distress since the Hamas-led attack.

articles.title: Vatican excommunicates breakaway Catholic bishops who defied Pope Leo XIV
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV excommunicates Society of St. Pius X bishops after unauthorized consecrations, declaring the traditionalist Catholic movement in schism.

articles.title: Khamenei body in cold storage as feared Basij mobilizes ahead of historic Iran funeral
articles.summary: Tehran prepares massive security operation for Ali Khamenei's July 9 burial, mobilizing Basij militia and IRGC for expected historic turnout.

articles.title: Families sue Maduro in US, accuse ex-president of directing police unit tied to extrajudicial killings
articles.summary: Families of five Venezuelan men filed a lawsuit accusing Nicolás Maduro of overseeing FAES, a police unit allegedly responsible for extrajudicial killings.

articles.title: One crew member missing after US Navy helicopter makes emergency landing in Arabian Sea
articles.summary: A U.S. Navy MH-60S Sea Hawk helicopter made an emergency water landing in the Arabian Sea, leaving one crew member missing from the USS George H.W. Bush.

articles.title: Russian generals' assassinations expose growing rift inside Putin's security apparatus
articles.summary: Internal frictions between the FSB and Russian military are growing as generals demand protection amid a wave of assassinations, sources say.

articles.title: Boy, 2, pulled alive from rubble six days after Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes
articles.summary: A 2-year-old boy named Klieber Moran was rescued after six days trapped beneath rubble in La Guaira, Venezuela, following devastating earthquakes.

articles.title: State Department congratulates Keiko Fujimori as Peru's president-elect following razor-thin vote count
articles.summary: The State Department congratulates Keiko Fujimori after she was declared winner of Peru's presidential runoff by fewer than 50,000 votes out of 18 million ballots cast.

articles.title: Fitness influencer says Lufthansa told her she looked ‘naked,’ forced her to zip up before boarding flight
articles.summary: Fitness influencer Edda Elisa Pilz says a Lufthansa employee called her "naked" and blocked her from boarding a flight over her athletic outfit.

articles.title: Thousands of police deployed across South Africa as deadly anti-immigration protests spread to multiple cities
articles.summary: Thousands of police deployed across South Africa as deadly anti-immigration protests spread across multiple cities, with looting and clashes reported.

articles.title: Wave of attacks on Iran's IRGC raises questions about renewed Kurdish insurgency
articles.summary: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces face deadly attacks across western Iran as clashes spread, raising questions about an escalating Kurdish insurgency amid U.S.–Iran talks.

articles.title: Nearly 100 monkeys escape enclosure, invade neighborhoods as officials scramble to recapture them
articles.summary: Nearly 100 macaques escaped a government enclosure in Lopburi, Thailand, prompting officials to use tranquilizer darts and baited cages to recapture them.

articles.summary: Bolivia's pro-U.S. government declares a state of emergency after 50 days of blockades led by former socialist President Evo Morales and his supporters.

articles.title: CEO’s fire death sparks mystery, fight over tech titan's fortune
articles.summary: Forensic ink analysis is underway on a mysterious will that arrived by mail at a Las Vegas courthouse, threatening to reshape the Zappos founder's estate.

articles.title: America's wounded cops find hope, healing and second chance through officers who refuse to leave them behind
articles.summary: Retired Las Vegas Lt. Randy Sutton, who had a career-ending stroke, founded the only nonprofit dedicated exclusively to wounded police officers.

articles.summary: A new app lets spectators track the Freedom 250 flyover schedule in real time, from the Blue Angels to the F-22 Raptor afterburner display.

articles.summary: A fast-moving storm capsized a boat Friday on Geneva Lake in Wisconsin, killing three people during the busy Fourth of July holiday weekend.

articles.title: US Coast Guard saves 8, including infant and child, after vessel capsizes off Fort Myers Beach
articles.summary: Video shows a Coast Guard rescue of eight people, including an infant, after a boat capsized near the Sanibel Causeway in southwest Florida.

articles.title: Military allies, historic tall ships converge on New Jersey shores to launch America 250 celebrations
articles.summary: Video captured roughly 20 nations' tall ships arriving at Sandy Hook as the Coast Guard Eagle prepares to lead a historic parade of ships Saturday.

articles.title: Police swarm Michigan shopping center after 'significant' shooting incident; at least 2 dead
articles.summary: Dearborn Police are actively investigating a shooting at the Fairlane Town Center mall. At least three people were shot, two fatally.

articles.title: US military deploys MTVRs, airlifts aid to Venezuela following deadly earthquakes
articles.summary: The USS Fort Lauderdale and Marine Corps vehicles support Venezuela earthquake relief as the death toll nears 2,000 and continues to rise.

articles.title: NYC rent board dissenter warns Mamdani-backed freeze could hurt affordable housing over time: 'Slow burn'
articles.summary: Rental Guidelines Board member Arpit Gupta said he disagrees with the freeze and favors a targeted approach that focuses more on low-income tenants.

articles.title: Philadelphia Salute to Independence parade canceled Friday as blistering temperatures slam the city
articles.summary: The Salute to Independence Semiquincentennial Parade scheduled to take place in Philadelphia was canceled Friday due to high temperatures.

articles.title: FBI announces 305 arrests, 24 missing children recovered in Chicago during Operation New Dawn
articles.summary: Operation New Dawn brought 11 federal agencies together to execute 305 arrests in Chicago over 60 days, recovering 24 kidnapped or missing children.

articles.title: Cities brace for holiday weekend teen takeovers putting bystanders at deadly risk, former prosecutor warns
articles.summary: Police departments are using drones, curfews and social media monitoring to prevent teen takeovers from disrupting the Fourth of July weekend.

articles.title: Trump kicks off Fourth of July weekend with symbolic salute to America’s legacy and more top headlines
articles.summary: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

articles.title: MMA fighter leans on faith in quest to catch suspected child predators
articles.summary: MMA fighter Dustin Lampros, known as 561 Predator Catcher, has completed about 100 catches of alleged child predators since 2022 in Florida.

articles.title: Maine man uses truck to rescue moose calf from charging bear: 'I knew what I had to do'
articles.summary: A Maine couple used their pickup truck to chase off a bear pursuing a moose calf during a fishing trip, and photos capture the dramatic wildlife encounter.

articles.title: Judge blocks Philadelphia law targeting masked federal officers
articles.summary: A federal judge blocked Philadelphia's mask ban on ICE agents, ruling the city cannot regulate how federal officers conduct enforcement operations.

articles.title: Massachusetts city imposes curfew ahead of historic World Cup match
articles.summary: Brockton officials announced an overnight curfew ahead of Cape Verde's World Cup match, citing shootings, reckless driving and public safety concerns.

articles.title: Fitness influencer pleads guilty in violent gym attack after beating man with weightlifting belt
articles.summary: Fitness influencer Wes Watson pleaded guilty to aggravated battery after footage showed him striking a man with a weightlifting belt at a Miami gym.

articles.title: Driver charged with manslaughter after Tesla crashes into Texas home, kills grandmother
articles.summary: Ring doorbell footage shows the Tesla Model 3 slamming into a Katy, Texas, home at 73 mph while the DoorDash driver claims Full Self-Driving was engaged.

articles.title: Mexican national made knife from pens, tried stabbing passenger in the brain on Vegas-bound flight, DOJ says
articles.summary: Mexican national Julio Alvarez Lopez pleaded guilty after prosecutors said he made a weapon from pens before a Seattle-to-Las Vegas flight and tried to stab a stranger through the eye.

articles.title: The crazy flag designs … that could have been our new flag
articles.summary: The story of the 1,900-plus designs submitted before President Dwight D. Eisenhower selected the United States of America's iconic 50-star flag.

articles.title: Ex-Texas middle school teacher sentenced to 33 years for sexually abusing 13-year-old student
articles.summary: Adriana Rullan, 30, was sentenced to 33 years after a Webb County jury convicted her of continuous sexual abuse of a 13-year-old Gonzalez Middle School student.
